example-hello-world.title: Hello World
example-hello-world.description: A single spinning cube with directional and ambient lighting — the simplest possible gpucat scene.

example-point-lights.title: Point Lights
example-point-lights.description: Coloured point lights in a struct storage buffer, orbiting a grid of spheres. The fragment loops over them and sums diffuse plus falloff. gpucat ships no lights; you write your own.

example-moving-mesh-stress.title: Moving Mesh Stress
example-moving-mesh-stress.description: 900 individually-animated meshes with per-frame quaternion updates and world matrix recalculations.

example-static-mesh-stress.title: Static Mesh Stress
example-static-mesh-stress.description: 900 static meshes with no per-frame updates — measures pure rendering overhead.

example-line.title: Line
example-line.description: Screen-space expanded lines using createLineGeometry and createLineMaterial. A static closed pentagon and an animated sine wave demonstrate fixed-pixel-width lines with updateLineGeometry for dynamic point updates.

example-texture.title: Texture
example-texture.description: A textured spinning box demonstrating user-provided Texture, UV coordinates, and texture sampling.

example-render-to-texture.title: Render to Texture
example-render-to-texture.description: Two-scene render-to-texture: an inner scene renders a textured spinning cube into an offscreen pass, then the outer scene samples the result onto a second cube.

example-mipmaps.title: Mipmaps
example-mipmaps.description: Side-by-side comparison of textures with and without mipmaps. Left plane uses mipmaps + 16x anisotropic filtering, right plane has no mipmaps — notice the aliasing in the distance.

example-instanced-mesh.title: Instanced Mesh
example-instanced-mesh.description: 30 instanced boxes with per-instance transforms and colours via InstancedBufferAttributeNode.

example-instancing-storage-buffer.title: Instancing with Storage Buffer
example-instancing-storage-buffer.description: 35 instanced boxes using storage buffers (mat4x4f transforms + vec4f colours) indexed by instance_index, combined with an instancedBufferAttribute for per-instance spin offset.

example-compute-particles.title: Compute Particles
example-compute-particles.description: 8192 GPU-simulated particles updated each frame by a @compute shader (renderer.compute) and rendered as instanced quads — no CPU readback.

example-compute-texture.title: Compute Texture
example-compute-texture.description: A compute shader writes an animated plasma into a storage texture (texture_storage_2d + textureStore), then a render pass samples that same texture onto a fullscreen quad — the canonical storage-texture write→sample round-trip.

example-video-texture.title: Video Texture
example-video-texture.description: An HTMLVideoElement used directly as a texture, mapped onto a spinning cube: each frame the texture is marked needsUpdate and the renderer copies the current video frame to the GPU. Clip: Big Buck Bunny (CC-BY, Blender Foundation).

example-volume.title: Volume
example-volume.description: A compute pass writes an animated value-noise density field into a 3D storage texture (texture_storage_3d + textureStore); a render pass raymarches its bounding box, sampling the same texture as a texture_3d with self-shadowing for a wispy, lit volumetric cloud. Drag to orbit.

example-ball-cluster.title: Ball Cluster
example-ball-cluster.description: Thousands of balls pulled toward the cursor and colliding into a packed cluster, simulated entirely on the GPU. Three compute passes per frame bin the balls into a spatial-hash grid, so each ball only tests its 27 neighbouring cells instead of every other ball.

example-indirect-batched.title: Indirect Batched
example-indirect-batched.description: CPU-driven indirect rendering: one merged geometry holds box and sphere data; two IndirectBuffers select sub-ranges via firstIndex + instanceCount. A slider moves the box/sphere split at runtime.

example-indirect-compute.title: Indirect Compute
example-indirect-compute.description: GPU-driven indirect rendering: a compute shader writes instanceCount directly into the indirect draw buffer each frame via IndirectBuffer.asStorageNode(), culling instances by distance — no CPU readback.

example-mrt.title: MRT (Multiple Render Targets)
example-mrt.description: Multiple Render Targets (MRT): renders a sphere to 4 targets (output, normal, diffuse, emissive) and composites them side-by-side for visualization.

example-raging-sea.title: Raging Sea
example-raging-sea.description: Animated ocean surface using sinusoidal large waves and multi-octave value noise small waves, with finite-difference normals and emissive wave crests — all computed on the GPU via vertex shader uniforms.

example-uniforms.title: Uniforms
example-uniforms.description: Demonstrates two uniform patterns: inline uniforms (shared across meshes) and name-based uniforms (per-material values resolved at render time).

example-storage.title: Storage Buffers
example-storage.description: Demonstrates two storage buffer patterns: inline storage (buffer passed directly) and name-based storage (resolved from geometry.buffers at render time).

example-voxels.title: Voxels
example-voxels.description: Procedurally generated voxel landscape using layered simplex noise, with culled meshing per chunk and per-vertex ambient occlusion computed on the CPU.

example-voxels-batched.title: Voxels Batched
example-voxels-batched.description: Batched voxel renderer with slab-allocated merged geometry, GPU frustum culling via compute shader, indirect draws, render distance management, and realtime voxel editing.

example-interleaved.title: Interleaved Vertex Buffers
example-interleaved.description: Demonstrates interleaved vertex attributes: normal and per-vertex color are packed into a single GpuBuffer with shared stride, producing one setVertexBuffer call for both attributes.

example-cubemap.title: Cube Texture Skybox
example-cubemap.description: Cube texture skybox rendered from inside a box. Each face has a distinct color and label to verify correct face mapping and cube texture sampling.

example-cube-camera.title: Cube Camera
example-cube-camera.description: A chrome sphere reflecting an animated environment in realtime. A CubeCamera renders the surroundings into the six faces of a CubeRenderTarget each frame, and the sphere samples that cube along its reflection vector.

example-discard.title: Discard
example-discard.description: Animated dissolve effect using the Discard() control flow node with a 4x4 Bayer dither matrix. Opacity oscillates via sin(time), discarding fragments below the dither threshold.

example-shadow-map.title: Shadow Map
example-shadow-map.description: Two-pass shadow mapping: a depth-only pass renders the scene from the light's orthographic camera into a depth texture, then the scene pass samples it with textureSampleCompare() to produce hard shadows.

example-array-texture.title: Array Texture Flipbook
example-array-texture.description: Flipbook animation using a 2D array texture. 16 procedurally generated frames are stored as layers in a DataArrayTexture; a uniform layer index cycles through them at 8 FPS.

example-fxaa.title: FXAA
example-fxaa.description: Fast Approximate Anti-Aliasing (FXAA) post-processing effect. Renders a high-contrast rotating cube to demonstrate edge smoothing compared to raw aliased output.

example-transform-controls.title: Transform Controls
example-transform-controls.description: Interactive translate/rotate/scale gizmo attached to scene objects. Press T/R/S to switch modes, Q to toggle world/local space, Tab to switch objects.

example-fly-controls.title: Fly Controls
example-fly-controls.description: First-person fly camera with WASD movement, right-click drag to look, Space/Shift for vertical movement, and scroll wheel to adjust speed.
